name: Akash Tyagi
northStar: midnight gallery wall with hot pink markers

dos: Use #000000 as the universal background for every screen — never introduce a lighter or colored canvas
Apply #ffa1f7 only to the single primary action per view; treat it as the system's only chromatic voice
Set section labels in Geist Pixel Triangle 30px with -0.033em tracking for editorial weight
Maintain 200px vertical gaps between major sections to preserve the gallery-wall rhythm
Use warm neutrals (#efe6d8 → #aca69c → #736e68 → #605c56) in descending tonal order for text hierarchy
Use the green dot (#4dff00) exclusively for live status/availability — never as a generic accent or button color
Render all project imagery edge-to-edge with no card chrome, radius, or shadow
donts: Never introduce box-shadows, gradients, or glow effects — flatness is the point
Never use #ffa1f7 for body text, backgrounds, or decorative purposes — it is an action color only
Never round image corners on project showcases — images must meet the canvas edge
Never add more than one filled CTA per screen — the system is single-action
Never use cool blue-grays or pure whites for text — the warm sepia palette is the identity
Never use icons in the header or footer — plain text links only
Never reduce section gaps below 104px — the generous whitespace is structural, not optional

colors.name: Obsidian Canvas
colors.role: Page background, card surfaces, inverted text on pink fills

colors.name: Parchment
colors.role: Primary body and heading text — warm near-white with sepia undertone, replaces the typical cool white of dark UIs

colors.name: Bone
colors.role: Secondary text, button borders, metadata — mid-tone warm gray

colors.name: Ash
colors.role: Muted body text, subtle link borders, inactive labels

colors.name: Charcoal
colors.role: Hairline dividers, low-emphasis borders, heading underlines on dark

colors.name: Neon Petal
colors.role: Primary action buttons, active nav states, selected markers, occasional heading accents — the single chromatic punctuation in the system

colors.name: Acid Lime
colors.role: Green wash for highlight backgrounds, decorative bands, and soft emphasis behind content. Use as a supporting accent, not as a status color

designSystem.layout: Full-bleed dark canvas with max-width 1400px centered content. The page reads as a vertical scroll of alternating text and image bands with extreme vertical breathing room (200px between sections). No sidebar, no sticky nav, no mega-menu — the header is a single static line. Hero pattern: two-column asymmetric text block (bio left, CTA right) on pure black, no hero image. Content arrangement alternates between text-only sections and full-bleed project images, creating a gallery-wall rhythm. Section labels ('SELECTED WORK 2015 — 2026') act as horizontal typographic dividers. The grid is implicit — no visible columns or gutters, content flows in max-width containers with generous margins. Navigation is reduced to name + time + email in the header and three text links in the footer.
designSystem.imagery: Product photography dominates — large, high-fidelity device mockups (tablets, phones) shot in lifestyle or twilight contexts with dramatic sky gradients. Images are full-bleed, occupying the full viewport width within the project showcase cards. No rounded corners, no masks, no frames — images meet the dark canvas edge-to-edge. No illustration or iconography beyond a single green status dot. The visual rhythm is: text block → silence → massive product photograph → silence → text block. Photography is the only visual content; UI screenshots inside product shots show dark UIs with warm text, mirroring the portfolio's own aesthetic.

similar.why: Dark portfolio canvas with single accent color, all-caps section labels, generous vertical spacing, and minimal navigation reduced to name and contact
Dark, near-black background with tight tracking, monospace metadata, and an editorial single-column scroll rhythm
Same flat-on-black surface treatment with no shadows or gradients, and tight negative letter-spacing on small body text
Personal portfolio with extreme whitespace, monospace timestamps, and text-only footer links without icons

surfaces.name: Canvas
surfaces.purpose: Full-bleed page background — true black for maximum contrast against warm text

surfaces.name: Project Surface
surfaces.purpose: Project image and content frames sit directly on canvas without card elevation

surfaces.name: Accent Surface
surfaces.purpose: Inverted pill buttons and selected markers — the only chromatic surface

designSystem.northStar: midnight gallery wall with hot pink markers

components.name: Pill CTA Button
components.description: Filled pill button, #ffa1f7 background, Obsidian (#000000) text, 40px border-radius, 12px vertical padding × 24px horizontal padding. Geist 500 at 13px. Used for the single most important action on screen (e.g. 'Book an intro call').

components.name: Ghost Text Link
components.description: No background, Parchment (#efe6d8) text with 1px Ash (#736e68) or Bone (#aca69c) border-bottom underline. Geist 500 at 13–14px. Underline appears only on hover, not at rest.

components.name: Status Availability Indicator
components.description: 8px Acid Lime (#4dff00) circle with 1000px border-radius, paired with Parchment (#efe6d8) text 'Available for work' at 13px Geist 500. The green dot is the only green element in the entire system.

components.name: Section Header with Date Range
components.description: Two-part label: section name left, date range right. Set in Geist Pixel Triangle 400 at 30px, Parchment (#efe6d8) for the label, Ash (#736e68) for the date range. Tight -0.033em letter-spacing. Separated from content by 104px of vertical space.

components.name: Project Showcase Card
components.description: Full-width project image displayed edge-to-edge on the Obsidian canvas with no card chrome. No border, no shadow, no radius — the image IS the card. Caption/description appears below in Geist 500, 14px, Parchment text.

components.name: Minimal Header Bar
components.description: Single-row layout: designer name left ('Akash Tyagi' in Geist 600, Parchment), current time and email right (Geist Mono 400, Ash). No logo, no nav links, no hamburger — the header is identity + clock, nothing more.

components.name: Two-Column Intro Block
components.description: Asymmetric two-column text block. Left column: condensed bio (2–3 short paragraphs). Right column: left empty or holds the CTA + status. Column gap 24–48px. Body text in Geist 500, 14px, Parchment with 16px paragraph spacing.

components.name: Footer Social Link
components.description: Plain text links — 'X.com', 'LinkedIn', 'Dribbble' — set in Geist Mono 400, 13px, Ash (#736e68) color. No icons, no badges. Separated by consistent 24px gaps.

typography.role: sans-serif — detected in extracted data but not described by AI
Primary UI and body typeface — medium and semibold weights, tight negative tracking at -0.013em to -0.031em. Used for body copy, links, navigation, and small headings.

Display and section-label typeface — pixel/decorative variant used for all-caps section headers like 'SELECTED WORK' and the year range '2015 — 2026'. The pixel treatment gives editorial weight without serifs.
Metadata typeface — timestamps, email, technical labels, year stamps. The monospace signals system/data language against the proportional body.

designSystem.description: Akash Tyagi is a dark editorial portfolio rendered as a midnight canvas with warm parchment text and a single vivid pink accent that acts as functional punctuation rather than decoration. The layout breathes — 200px section gaps, generous asymmetric two-column compositions, and display labels set in Geist Pixel Triangle create a gallery-wall rhythm where each project is given the silence it needs. Every neutral carries a warm sepia undertone, deliberately steering away from the cool blue-grays of typical tech portfolios. Components are minimal and flat: pill CTAs, hairline dividers, status dots, and large unfettered project imagery — there is no elevation, no gradients, no ornamentation beyond typography and a single chromatic accent.

system"}],"datePublished":"2026-05-10T22:34:05.662Z","dateModified":"2026-05-10T22:34:05.662Z"}]}19:T453,Quick Color Reference:
- text: #efe6d8 (Parchment)
- background: #000000 (Obsidian)
- border: #605c56 (Charcoal) / #736e68 (Ash)
- accent: #ffa1f7 (Neon Petal)
- status: #4dff00 (Acid Lime) — availability dot only
- primary action: #ffa1f7 (filled action)

Example Component Prompts:

1. Create a section header: 200px top padding. Left-aligned label 'SELECTED WORK' in Geist Pixel Triangle 30px, #efe6d8, letter-spacing -1px. Right-aligned date range '2015 — 2026' in Geist Mono 13px, #736e68.

2. Create a status row: 8px green circle (#4dff00, 1000px radius) followed by 'Available for work' in Geist 500, 14px, #efe6d8, separated by 8px gap.

3. Create a pill CTA: 40px border-radius, #ffa1f7 background, 12px 24px padding, 'Book an intro call' in Geist 500 13px, #000000 text, no border.

4. Create a footer link row: three text links 'X.com', 'LinkedIn', 'Dribbble' in Geist Mono 400, 13px, #736e68, 24px gap between each, left-aligned.

5. Create a project showcase: full-width image edge-to-edge on #000000 canvas, no border or radius, 104px top padding, caption below in Geist 500 14px #efe6d8.

designSystem.elevationPhilosophy: No shadows. Surfaces are separated purely by tone and whitespace. Elevation is expressed through typographic scale and generous spacing, not box-shadow stacks. This flatness is deliberate — shadows would introduce visual noise on a portfolio that treats each project as a gallery frame.
